stocks.news: Costco (COST +0.84%) now makes its own energy drink customers swear tastes just like Celsius (CELH -6.57%), whose stock tanked on the news.

economy.commentary:
Young Graduates Face the Grimmest Job Market in Years: The jobless rate for young grads hit 5.6%, above the overall rate of 4.2% for all workers.

Hegseth 'disappointed' by idea of ceasefire with Iran, says Trump.

crypto.commentary: Circle stock plunges 20% as a new draft of the Clarity Act threatens stablecoin rewards.

real_estate.homebuilder: The National Association of Home Builders (NAHB) reports that multifamily "missing middle" construction is seeing its best year since 2007.
real_estate.commentary:
The Bay Area is experiencing a "Spring Reset" where inventory is building faster than sales are closing. Santa Clara County is a notable outlier with a 3.5% year-over-year price decline, providing a strategic window for buyers to negotiate as active listings have more than doubled since December.

📍 Latest Real Estate News in the Bay Area: The Bay Area spring market is currently characterized by a widening gap between available inventory and actual closings. In Santa Clara County, active single-family listings have surged to 1,186—more than double December's levels—while actual sales fell 42% year-over-year. This has led to a modest 3.5% price dip in the county, contrasting with San Francisco's 7.7% appreciation. In Alameda County, the market remains the most accessible entry point with a median price of $925,000, and while it is still considered a seller's market, homes are staying on the market longer (median 26 days) as buyers become more selective.

📍 Latest General News in the Bay Area: In Milpitas, local focus remains on surging gas prices, which have prompted the Valley Transportation Authority (VTA) to urge residents to switch to public transit as an eco-friendly alternative. Residents in Santa Clara County are also tracking a new state holiday; Assembly Speaker Robert Rivas officially announced California Farmworkers Day to honor labor history. In Fremont, the community is welcoming new Police Chief Floyd Mitchell, who officially began his tenure this week. Additionally, Fremont police are reminding residents planning spring travel to utilize their free "Home Check" property surveillance programs for added security while away.
